title: Two Sum
prompt: Given an array of integers `nums` and an integer `target`, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to `target`.
sampleExplanation: Given nums = [2, 7, 11, 15], target = 9. Because nums[0] + nums[1] == 9, return [0, 1].

python.driver:
import sys
import json

if not hasattr(Solution, 'twoSum'):
    print("SIGNATURE_ERROR: Method 'twoSum' not found in class 'Solution'", file=sys.stderr)
    sys.exit(1)

try:
    input_data = sys.stdin.read().strip()
    args = json.loads(input_data) if input_data else []
    sol = Solution()
    res = sol.twoSum(*args)
    print(json.dumps(res))
except Exception as e:
    print(f"RUNTIME_ERROR: {str(e)}", file=sys.stderr)
    sys.exit(1)

hints: Use a hash map to look up complements in O(1) time.

title: Fibonacci Number
prompt: Calculate the N-th Fibonacci number. F(0) = 0, F(1) = 1, F(N) = F(N-1) + F(N-2) for N > 1.

python.driver:
import sys
import json

if not hasattr(Solution, 'fib'):
    print("SIGNATURE_ERROR: Method 'fib' not found in class 'Solution'", file=sys.stderr)
    sys.exit(1)

try:
    input_data = sys.stdin.read().strip()
    args = json.loads(input_data) if input_data else []
    sol = Solution()
    res = sol.fib(*args)
    print(json.dumps(res))
except Exception as e:
    print(f"RUNTIME_ERROR: {str(e)}", file=sys.stderr)
    sys.exit(1)

hints: Can be solved in O(n) time and O(1) space.
